speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got a great eye — this 2018 Camry LE is one of the cleanest we've had come through in months. Just 78,000 miles on Toyota's bulletproof 2.5L engine, fully serviced with fresh oil, and you know what they say about Camrys: this thing will run to 300K without breaking a sweat. We've got it priced at $18,495, which is a steal considering what these are going for right now. Want to take a closer look?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Thanks, it looks great in the pictures. Has it been in any accidents?

text: Thanks, it looks great in the pictures. Has it been in any accidents?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great question — and I'm happy to tell you this one's clean as a whistle. No accidents on the Carfax, no frame damage, nothing structural whatsoever. It's a one-owner vehicle that was clearly babied. You can see for yourself when you check it out in person — the body lines are all straight, paint's consistent all around. This is exactly the kind of history you want when you're buying a used Camry. Were you looking to come by today and take it for a spin?

args.message: That's great to hear! It sounds like a solid car. I'm willing to offer $18,200 for it.

text: That's great to hear! It sounds like a solid car. I'm willing to offer $18,200 for it.

speaker: seller

args.message: I appreciate the offer! You're clearly serious, and I respect that. Look, I'll be straight with you — we just had a 2018 Camry LE with 85,000 miles go at auction last week for over $18,500, and that one didn't have the clean history this one does. I can come down a little to $18,350, but that's about as low as I can go and still keep my manager happy. At that price, you're getting a fantastic deal on a car that's going to serve you well for years. What do you say?
